evals.prompt: Implement the ToBe Vue 3 + TypeScript version of the Find Owners screen. The As-Is artifacts are already available at tests/e2e/find-owners.spec.ts and docs/modernization/find-owners-screen-spec.md and docs/modernization/find-owners-preserve-vs-change.md. The new implementation must be fully JSP-independent, preserve URL compatibility (/owners/find, /owners), and pass all existing Playwright E2E tests. Use Vue 3 Composition API with TypeScript strict mode.
evals.expected_output: Should produce Vue 3 SFC components for the Find Owners screen (search form + results table), TypeScript interfaces, API client, Vue Router config, Vite build config, and any necessary Spring REST controller additions. All existing Playwright E2E tests at tests/e2e/find-owners.spec.ts must pass. Backend tests (./mvnw -q test) must also pass. A structured migration summary must be provided.
expectations: Vue 3 SFC component files are created under src/main/frontend/
Components use <script setup lang="ts"> syntax (Composition API)
TypeScript interfaces are defined for Owner data
The implementation does not embed Vue inside a JSP page as the final solution
The screen spec at docs/modernization/find-owners-screen-spec.md is read and referenced
The preserve-vs-change log at docs/modernization/find-owners-preserve-vs-change.md is read
Playwright E2E tests are executed with: npx playwright test tests/e2e/find-owners.spec.ts
Backend tests are executed with: ./mvnw -q test
URL paths /owners/find and /owners are preserved
A final migration summary is provided in the required format

evals.prompt: Now implement the ToBe Vue 3 + TypeScript version of the Owner Details screen. This is a horizontal expansion from the Find Owners migration. Use the same frontend architecture established for Find Owners. Read the As-Is artifacts at tests/e2e/owner-details.spec.ts and docs/modernization/owner-details-screen-spec.md. The owner details page shows owner info, their pets, visit history, and has links to edit owner, add pet, edit pet, and add visit.
evals.expected_output: Should produce Vue 3 components for the Owner Details screen that show owner information, pet listing with visit history, and all navigation links (edit owner, add/edit pet, add visit). Components should integrate with the existing frontend architecture from Find Owners. Must pass E2E tests and backend tests.
expectations: Vue components are created for Owner Details display
Components show owner info, pet listing, and visit history
Navigation links for edit/add operations are present
The implementation follows the same architecture as Find Owners (reuses types, API patterns)
As-Is screen spec is read before implementation begins
Missing As-Is artifacts are reported if they don't exist
A migration summary in the required format is produced
Verification commands are executed (Playwright + Maven)

evals.prompt: Implement the ToBe Vue 3 + TypeScript version of the Add/Edit Pet form. This is a form-heavy screen with pet name input, birth date picker, and pet type dropdown. Use the existing frontend architecture. Read As-Is artifacts. The form must validate required fields and show error messages matching the legacy behavior exactly.
evals.expected_output: Should produce Vue 3 form components with proper form validation, date picker integration, and pet type dropdown populated from the API. Must handle both create and edit modes. Server-side validation messages must be displayed. Must attempt to run E2E tests and backend tests.
expectations: Vue component handles both add and edit pet flows
Form includes name, birth date, and pet type fields
TypeScript interfaces are defined for Pet and PetType
Form validation displays error messages matching legacy behavior
Missing As-Is artifacts trigger the input-missing protocol
Verification commands are attempted
A migration summary is produced

evals.prompt: Implement the ToBe Vue 3 + TypeScript version of Find Owners. When the first Playwright test run fails, diagnose the failures, fix the Vue implementation, and re-run until tests pass. Show me the full repair loop with what failed and what you fixed at each step.
evals.expected_output: Should demonstrate the repair loop: initial implementation, first test run with failures, diagnosis of each failure, fixes applied, re-run showing progress, and final passing state. The repair log should be detailed with failure reasons and fix descriptions.
expectations: Playwright E2E tests are run at least once
A locator map is built by studying the test file before implementing
If failures occur, failure output is analyzed with specific reasons identified
A repair log table is included in the final summary (even if empty with explanation)
The fix-rerun loop continues until tests pass or blockers are reported
The final summary explains the prevention strategy or repair iterations

evals.prompt: Implement the ToBe Vue 3 + TypeScript version of the Vet List screen. Note: there are no As-Is artifacts yet for this screen - no tests/e2e/vet-list.spec.ts and no docs/modernization/vet-list-screen-spec.md exist. Handle this situation according to your input contract.
evals.expected_output: Should detect that required As-Is artifacts are missing, stop implementation, report exactly which artifacts are missing and their expected paths, and provide a ready-to-run prompt for the modernization-asis-test-guardrails skill to generate them. Should NOT proceed with implementation without the artifacts.
expectations: The agent checks for tests/e2e/vet-list.spec.ts and reports it missing
The agent checks for docs/modernization/vet-list-screen-spec.md and reports it missing
Implementation is NOT started without As-Is artifacts
A ready-to-run prompt for modernization-asis-test-guardrails is provided
The response clearly states which artifacts are required before proceeding
The agent does not guess at screen behavior
